title: Kokos MCP Server tools.yaml
description: JSON Schema for the MCP server tools DSL. Top-level keys are capability groups (e.g. 'employees', 'deliverables'), each containing a map of tool-key → ToolYaml definitions.

additionalProperties.description: A capability group. Keys are arbitrary tool identifiers (snake_case by convention); values are ToolYaml definitions.

Query.description: Configuration for GraphQL-based tools. When this block is present the factory creates a DefaultGraphQLToolSpec.

graphql.description: Full GraphQL query string to execute against the Sme.UP GraphQL endpoint.

Response.description: Controls how the raw Sme.UP response is mapped to the MCP tool result returned to the LLM client. The 'format' field is the primary configuration point: it defines a template that is applied once per row (EXB) or per item (GraphQL) to produce the text sent to the LLM.

format.description:
Per-row output template applied by SmeupDataTableMapper (EXB/FUN tools) or by the GraphQL mapper.

EXB tools (fun field present, no success_result):
  Use '${COLUMN_TITLE}' named placeholders, where COLUMN_TITLE is the human-readable column title as returned by the Sme.UP backend — NOT the technical column name (e.g. use '${Descrizione}' not '${£095R_DE}'). Placeholder keys are matched case-sensitively against the 'title' field of each column in the response.
  Multi-line titles (containing a real newline) must be written with the literal two-character sequence '\n' in the YAML scalar (e.g. '${Data\nFine\nprevista}' for a title that spans three lines). The mapper normalises these before lookup.
  If a placeholder does not match any column title, the value is resolved by column name as a fallback, then replaced with an empty string if still not found.
  When no 'format' is set, the mapper emits one 'Title: value' line per column (default formatting).

GraphQL tools (query.graphql field present):
  Use a printf-style '%s' placeholder; the entire raw GraphQL response item is interpolated at that position.

error_format.description: Template applied when the API call fails. Supports a single printf-style '%s' placeholder that receives the raw error detail. Used for both GraphQL and FUN errors. If omitted, the raw error message is returned as-is.

success_message.description: Human-readable message returned to the LLM on successful LOA40_SI (write) operations, in place of the raw backend response. Supports an optional '%s' placeholder substituted with the affected object's identifier. Used only when 'success_result' is also set.

error_message.description: Human-readable message returned to the LLM when a LOA40_SI (write) operation fails. Supports an optional '%s' placeholder for the error detail. Used only when 'success_result' is also set.

success_result.description: Expected value of the 'Return Code' field in a LOA40_SI response that signals success (e.g. '*SUCCESS'). When this field is present the factory selects DefaultLOA40ToolSpec, which validates the backend response against this value and returns 'success_message' on match or 'error_message' otherwise. Omit for read-only EXB/GraphQL tools.

Tree.description: Enables hierarchical (tree) rendering for TRE-type FUN responses. When 'enabled' is true the response is processed by SmeupDataTreeMapper instead of the flat-table mapper.

enabled.description: Activates tree-mode rendering. Must be true for all other tree options to take effect.

mime_type.description: MIME type declared for the tree content returned to the client.

children_field.description: Name of the field in each tree node that holds its child nodes.

level_mappings.description: Maps each tree depth level (0-based integer key) to a set of field extraction rules. Each level object maps Sme.UP column names to the output field names ('value', 'id', 'obj', etc.). Allows different extraction logic per depth.

ToolYaml.description: Definition of a single MCP tool. The factory auto-selects the implementation class based on which fields are present: custom 'class' → reflection instantiation; 'query.graphql' → DefaultGraphQLToolSpec; 'fun' + 'response.success_result' → DefaultLOA40ToolSpec; 'fun' only → DefaultToolSpec.

name.description: Display name exposed to the MCP client (and therefore to the LLM). Use kebab-case (e.g. 'get-deliverables-actives').

description.description: Human-readable description of what the tool does. This text is passed to the LLM so it should be clear and precise to help the model decide when to invoke the tool.
examples: Return the list of all Sme.UP Lab deliverables currently in active state.
Modifies a Sme.UP object instance using LOA40.

schema.description: JSON Schema string (serialised as a YAML block scalar) defining the tool's input parameters. The 'properties' map declares each argument; 'required' lists mandatory ones. Placeholders referenced in 'fun' or 'response.format' must match property names here.

class.description: Fully qualified Java class name of a custom ToolSpec implementation built into the MCP server itself. IMPORTANT: this field is only meaningful when the tool definition lives inside the MCP server project, where the referenced class is on the classpath. If you are writing a tools.yaml for an external micro-executor (the common case), this field is NOT usable — the class would not be available at runtime. For custom behaviour in an external micro-executor, implement a KokosService that wraps your logic and exposes it via a FUN, then target that FUN with the 'fun' field as usual. Omit or set to 'DEFAULT' for automatic dispatch.

fun.description: Sme.UP FUN command template. Use '${paramName}' named placeholders that correspond to tool input arguments declared in 'schema'. The FUN type prefix (EXB, TRE, LOA40_SI, …) determines which response mapper is used.
